mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following rate cuts are not statistically significantly positive (p > 0.05) over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce corporate earnings expectations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of instances when the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.2 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength, which sustains corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal yields a negative average 60-day forward return or a Rank IC below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects supply-side constraints that compress profit margins, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Industrial production declines during tightening cycles are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of observed cases.

mechanism_narrative: A falling federal funds rate combined with stable consumer price inflation indicates accommodative monetary policy without inflationary pressure, which reliably generates positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The combined signal of rate cuts and stable CPI produces negative 20-day forward returns in > 50% of triggered months over a 4-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month declines in total nonfarm payrolls directly reduce aggregate consumption capacity, leading to lower corporate earnings forecasts and negative S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Payroll declines are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in > 55% of cases, or the signal Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 2.5 percent signals robust economic expansion that boosts equity risk appetite, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns following GDP growth above 2.5 percent are negative in > 50% of quarters, or the signal shows no statistical edge (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between falling industrial production and rising retail sales during a supply-constrained regime indicates inventory depletion and margin compression, driving negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: The divergence signal yields positive 20-day forward returns in > 60% of occurrences, or the interaction term loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02) in walk-forward tests.
